homework.title: Identifying Chemical Properties
homework.instructions: Select the correct option that best describes the property mentioned.
homework.question: Which of the following is a physical property of sodium?
options: It has a low melting point and is soft
It forms an oxide layer instantly
It conducts electricity only when molten

homework.title: Acids and Bases in Daily Life
homework.instructions: Identify the substance and explain its nature based on the description.
homework.question: A common household cleaning agent turns red litmus paper blue. Another substance used in antacids tastes bitter. Identify the nature (acidic or basic) of both substances and give one example of each from your home.
homework.answerSpace: Write a short paragraph explaining your answer.
homework.expectedResponse: The cleaning agent is basic (e.g., Soap). The antacid substance is basic (e.g., Magnesium Hydroxide). Both turn red litmus blue and taste bitter.

homework.title: Reaction Observation
homework.instructions: Draw a labelled diagram showing the setup for the reaction between Zinc and dilute Hydrochloric acid. Label the reactants, products, and the gas evolved.
homework.question: Describe the observation when Zinc granules are added to dilute HCl. Draw the experimental setup and label the gas collected.
homework.answerSpace: Draw the diagram and write the observation below it.
homework.visualRequirement: A labelled diagram of a test tube with a delivery tube leading to an inverted jar or water displacement setup.
homework.expectedResponse: Diagram showing Zn + HCl -> ZnCl2 + H2. Observation: Effervescence (bubbles) due to Hydrogen gas evolution.

homework.title: pH Scale Application
homework.instructions: Analyze the given pH values and answer the questions regarding acidity.
homework.question: Solution A has a pH of 2. Solution B has a pH of 10. Solution C has a pH of 7. (a) Which solution is strongly acidic? (b) Which solution is neutral? (c) Arrange them in increasing order of hydrogen ion concentration [H+].
homework.expectedResponse: (a) Solution A. (b) Solution C. (c) B < C < A (in terms of H+ concentration, actually A > C > B).

homework.title: Corrosion and Protection
homework.instructions: Read the scenario and answer the questions based on chemical principles.
homework.question: An iron gate in a coastal area rusts quickly, while the same gate in a dry desert remains shiny for years. (a) Identify the chemical process responsible for rusting. (b) Explain why the coastal environment accelerates this process. (c) Suggest two methods to prevent rusting on the gate.
homework.expectedResponse: (a) Oxidation/Corrosion. (b) Presence of moisture and salt in coastal air acts as an electrolyte, speeding up the reaction. (c) Painting, Galvanization, or Oiling.

homework.title: Natural Indicators at Home
homework.instructions: Perform a safe home activity using common items to identify acidic or basic substances.
homework.question: Collect turmeric powder or red cabbage water. Test it with lemon juice, soap solution, and vinegar. Record the color changes observed for each substance and classify them as acidic or basic.
homework.answerSpace: Create a table with columns: Substance, Indicator Color Change, Nature (Acid/Base).
homework.expectedResponse: Turmeric turns red in soap (Basic); Lemon juice and vinegar show no change or specific color depending on the indicator used.

learningOutcomesCovered: LO_01: Identify physical and chemical properties of substances
LO_02: Distinguish between acids and bases using indicators and real-life examples
LO_03: Observe and record chemical reactions
LO_04: Apply pH scale concepts to solve problems
LO_05: Understand corrosion and methods of prevention
